title: Scene Mood
description: Evocative words for the emotional tone or atmosphere of a scene, each with associations to set the mood.
entries: WISTFUL — yearning, faraway, aching for what was
OMINOUS — foreboding, threatening, heavy with dread
BLISSFUL — rapturous, floating, lost in contentment
FRANTIC — frenzied, breathless, spinning out of hand
MELANCHOLY — sorrowful, blue, wrapped in quiet grief
GIDDY — dizzy, elated, bubbling over with joy
TENSE — taut, coiled, ready to snap
DREAMLIKE — hazy, floating, unmoored from the real
BLEAK — desolate, grim, drained of all hope
TRIUMPHANT — soaring, jubilant, flush with victory
EERIE — uncanny, spectral, wrong in a soft way
COZY — snug, warm, sheltered from the cold
SUSPENSEFUL — held-breath, waiting, edge of the seat
MOURNFUL — grieving, plaintive, thick with loss
PLAYFUL — light, teasing, sparkling with fun
SOLEMN — grave, reverent, hushed with weight
CHAOTIC — riotous, unruly, tumbling apart
ROMANTIC — tender, glowing, charged with longing
SINISTER — menacing, dark, veiled in bad intent
NOSTALGIC — sentimental, backward-looking, warm and worn
FEVERISH — hot, restless, burning at the edges
TRANQUIL — placid, gentle, deeply at rest
FORLORN — abandoned, lonely, left to the dark
EXHILARATING — thrilling, electric, wind in the blood
GLOOMY — overcast, dim, sagging with weight
SACRED — hallowed, holy, humming with awe
PARANOID — watchful, jittery, sure of unseen eyes
JUBILANT — rejoicing, festive, loud with delight
DESOLATE — barren, forsaken, empty to the horizon
SULTRY — languid, heavy, warm and slow
MENACING — looming, dangerous, a coiled fist
HOPEFUL — bright, rising, tilted toward dawn
MOROSE — sullen, brooding, sunk in gloom
FRENZIED — wild, thrashing, past all restraint
PENSIVE — thoughtful, inward, lost in reflection
HAUNTED — troubled, shadowed, chased by the past
CAROUSING — rowdy, merry, drunk on the night
STIFLING — smothering, airless, pressing in close
WONDROUS — marvelous, agog, brimming with awe
DREARY — dull, gray, drizzling with tedium
ELECTRIC — crackling, alive, humming with charge
MELLOW — soft, easy, warm as low light
ANXIOUS — uneasy, fretful, knotted with worry
GRAND — sweeping, majestic, larger than life
SUBDUED — muted, quieted, dialed low
